title: Shawia viscosa
intro: Shawia viscosa, also known as African rue or stick fig, is a plant species in the family Apocynaceae, native to Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Shawia viscosa include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Shawia viscosa is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is commonly used to treat fever, pain, and skin conditions.
